title: SMOTE: Synthetic minority over-sampling technique
methodology: Algorithmic description + benchmarks on nine imbalanced UCI datasets vs naive over- and under-sampling.
contributions: Synthesise minority-class examples by interpolating between each minority instance and its k nearest neighbours of the same class.
Combined with majority under-sampling, SMOTE outperforms naive over-sampling (which only duplicates) on a range of imbalanced classification benchmarks.
weaknesses: Can generate implausible synthetic minority points in high-dimensional or strongly non-linear feature spaces.
Originally numeric-only; SMOTE-NC extends it to mixed types.
Does not interact well with overlapping class regions (where minority points lie in majority territory).
emerald_relevance: Foundational citation for section 4.4 + the SMOTE-NC variant used in section 5.7 for mixed numeric/categorical resampling on green-loan data.
notes: Foundational SMOTE paper. Cited 25k+. SMOTE-NC variant handles mixed numeric/categorical.
